summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire, rare Reuben & Cherise, solid second set
blurb: The show delivers a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition, followed by a seamless Estimated Prophet → Uncle John's Band → Drums → Space sequence. Reuben And Cherise opens the first set with rare energy and Jerry’s vocals are extra‑special. Overall performance is strong despite some keyboard texture issues.
review: On March 27 1991 at Nassau Coliseum the Dead rolled out a set that still feels fresh for anyone hunting that early‑’90s magic. The first set kicks off with a hard‑hitting Hell In A Bucket and rolls into an unexpected Reuben And Cherise, a rarity that had the island crowd buzzing – reviewers note Jerry’s “extra special” vocal push on the outro. The second set is where the night truly shines: a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue erupts into a fluid Estimated Prophet → Uncle John's Band chain, then Bill Kreutzmann lights up Drums → Space with a bass‑solo‑laden interlude before launching into an electrifying All Along The Watchtower and a heartfelt Stella Blue that rolls straight into Sugar Magnolia. Phil’s bass on New Minglewood Blues is described as crystal‑clear and driving, while Vince’s synth/organ tones divide opinion – some call it “carnival organ” but others enjoy the extra texture. Despite hiss and occasional thinness in the SBD mix, the performance captures a band locked in, delivering both rarity (Reuben & Cherise) and classic fire (Scarlet>Fire).

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are mixed and one is positive; overall reviewers praise the set’s energy, rare Reuben & Cherise, Scarlet>Fire and strong drumming while noting keyboard quirks. The consensus leans slightly positive, warranting a modest +0.4 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture that highlights the rare Rubin/Cherise and scorching Scarlet>Fire despite minor hiss; most complete and clear version available.

key_highlights: Rare Reuben & Cherise opener energizes the first set
Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition
Seamless second‑set flow through Estimated Prophet, Uncle John's Band, Drums → Space and Watchtower
Emotive Stella Blue and strong Phil Lesh bass on New Minglewood Blues

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers strong lead work with his ‘Wolf’ guitar and extra‑special vocals, especially on the Reuben outro.
band_performance.Phil: Provides clear, driving bass—highlighted on New Minglewood Blues—and is praised for sounding great across recordings.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm playing; vocal level noted as a bit loud by one reviewer but otherwise steady.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard tone divides opinion: described as ‘carnival organ’ and harsh, yet some enjoy the added texture; timing issues noted on Scarlet jam.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides supportive percussion throughout with no notable criticisms.
band_performance.Billy: Shines in the Drums → Space segment, delivering a standout bass‑solo‑filled space jam.
